content.assessment: Grade-splitting operates within the existing tankerület authority structure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01) and directly targets the national funding-formula pressure by concentrating upper-grade enrollment where minimum class-size thresholds are more achievable (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04). However, this proposal does not alter who decides consolidation site placement, nor address the underlying coverage crisis affecting settlements with no institution at all (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06); it is a within-system reallocation, not a governance change. The historically documented within-school segregation-limiting effect of small single-grade schools (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05, contested) could be partially preserved for lower grades but is at risk for upper grades once consolidated.
opportunities: Could interact constructively with the 2026 governance reform's restored principal autonomy, since local school leaders may be positioned to negotiate which grades remain (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07)
strengths: Directly engages the identified structural fiscal driver — national class-size/per-pupil funding thresholds — by concentrating enrollment where thresholds are more feasible (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04)
Fully compatible with existing tankerület decision authority; requires no change to who holds closure/reorganization power (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01)
threats: Upper-grade consolidation could disrupt the historical within-school segregation-limiting pattern attributed to small single-class schools, an effect that is itself contested (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05)

Kindergarten coverage gaps governed by similarly rigid enrollment thresholds (F-live-hungarian_education_system-08) are untouched by a primary-grade-focused mechanism, leaving compounding early-years access problems unresolved
weaknesses: Does not address settlements already outside any institutional network (38% coverage gap), since it presumes an existing local school to split rather than restoring provision (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06)
Leaves discretionary closure-adjacent decision-making (which grades/villages get merged) entirely within tankerület purview without added procedural safeguards (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03)
